title: 3-year-old rape-murder case in Pune: Massive protest on Mumbai-Bengaluru highway; traffic hit
content: Protesters blocked the Mumbai–Bengaluru highway in Pune for nearly four hours following the rape and murder of a three-year-old girl. Demonstrators placed the child's body on the road, demanding strict punishment for the 65-year-old accused, who has a prior criminal record. Authorities have assured a fast-track trial and the harshest punishment for the perpetrator.

title: 3am AC blast? Short circuit? How fire raced through locked, grilled Delhi Shahdara flats; 9 dead
content: A devastating fire, possibly triggered by an AC blast or short circuit, swept through a four-storey building in Vivek Vihar, east Delhi, killing nine people. The blaze, which started around 3:47 am, rapidly spread, trapping residents behind locked doors and grilled windows. Firefighters rescued about 15 people, but nine bodies were recovered from various floors.

title: Chants of power: How slogans set the tone in Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam elections
content: Political parties in India's recent assembly elections heavily relied on catchy slogans to energize campaigns and shape narratives. From West Bengal's "Bhoy out, Bharosa in" to Tamil Nadu's "Stalin Thodaratum, Tamil Nadu Vellattum," these short phrases became central to electoral battles, reflecting regional identities and key campaign themes.

title: IPL playoff scenarios: CSK still alive but margin for error is shrinking
content: Chennai Super Kings reignited their IPL 2026 campaign with a crucial eight-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians. This win, driven by disciplined bowling and a steady chase led by Gaikwad and Sharma, keeps their playoff hopes alive. However, their IPL 2026 campaign has been marred by inconsistency and CSK must now string together wins in their remaining five matches to secure a top-four spot.

title: The $900B reason Anthropic CEO keeps talking about AI taking away millions of jobs
content: nthropic CEO Dario Amodei keeps predicting that AI will trigger mass unemployment on a global scale. But with an Anthropic IPO reportedly targeting a $900 billion valuation, his warnings may be doing double duty—as both public service and investor pitch. Anthropic's own labor research shows the reality is far more gradual. And voices from Sam Altman to Meta's Yann LeCun are increasingly pushing back.

title: Man poisons family of five in Gurgaon, attempts suicide
content: A man in Gurgaon allegedly poisoned his wife and four children before attempting suicide. Police discovered the family deceased in their Wazirpur village home late Saturday night. The accused, hospitalized and unfit for questioning, is being treated while authorities investigate the motive behind the tragic incident.

title: ‘I'm vegetarian, need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: 9 killed in residential building fire in Delhi’s Shahdara; AC blast suspected
content: Tragedy struck Shahdara early Sunday as a devastating fire ripped through a residential building, claiming at least nine lives. Firefighters battled the inferno for hours after a distress call at 3:27 am. While preliminary reports point to a short circuit, the exact cause remains under investigation. Many were hospitalized following the harrowing incident.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 45: Ranveer Singh’s film eyes Rs 1790 cr globally
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its phenomenal run, crossing Rs 1,138.54 crore in India net collections after 45 days. The film's 7th Saturday saw a significant surge, earning Rs 2.40 crore, pushing its gross worldwide total to Rs 1,788.32 crore. This success comes despite new releases, proving its enduring appeal.

title: After over 20K layoffs, how Oracle's 'promise' to Sam Altman is hurting banks across US
content: Oracle faces a significant bank-debt challenge due to its massive data centre expansion for OpenAI, reportedly requiring $300 billion. Lenders struggle with exposure limits, impacting their ability to finance these projects.  This comes as Oracle also plans substantial job cuts, highlighting financial pressures despite efforts to secure funding.

title: Hyderabad man tells wife her mother died from seizure on bike ride; CCTV shows he hit her with helmet
content: A 19-year-old woman's suspicion led to her husband's arrest for culpable homicide after he allegedly struck his mother-in-law, causing her fatal head injuries. The accused initially claimed the victim suffered an epileptic seizure, but CCTV footage revealed he hit her during an argument on his bike.

title: With just 36 hours to exam, NEET aspirant’s dream buried under rubble in MP
content: A 17-year-old aspiring doctor's dreams were crushed as her home was demolished just 36 hours before her NEET exam. Ankita Dangi, an OBC candidate, lost her study materials and identity documents in the sudden demolition of 27 homes. Now seeking refuge in a temple, she faces challenges meeting exam dress code and identification requirements.

title: Karnataka: Man kills 2 minor sons, dies by suicide after dispute over wife’s alleged affair
content: A tragic incident unfolded in Tumakuru, Karnataka, where a 40-year-old man allegedly murdered his two young sons before taking his own life. Police suspect marital discord stemming from his wife's alleged affair as the motive.  Despite prior intervention, the situation escalated, leading to this devastating outcome. Investigations are ongoing.

title: '10 lifetimes won't be enough': TMC dares BJP to contest from Falta after repolling announced
content: TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee challenged the BJP and Election Commission to contest from Falta after a repoll was announced due to "severe electoral offences." The Election Commission cited widespread tampering of EVMs and compromised integrity of recorded votes at multiple booths, leading to the decision for a fresh poll on May 21.

title: What's going wrong for Jasprit Bumrah, Surya, Tilak Verma?
content: Mumbai Indians head coach Mahela Jayawardene expressed confidence in his senior players despite a challenging IPL 2026 season. He backed Suryakumar Yadav and Tilak Varma, emphasizing patience and development amidst the team's struggles at the bottom of the points table.

title: 'In uniform you are in service': Motorist who took on minister in traffic jam tells her story
content: Teena Choudhary, a Worli resident, revealed her identity after confronting minister Girish Mahajan during a reservation agitation traffic jam. She explained her frustration at being stuck for 90 minutes while trying to pick up her daughter. Choudhary praised Mahajan for listening and resolving the issue, while urging political parties to address public apathy.

title: IPL playoff qualification scenarios: Time is running out for Mumbai Indians
content: The Mumbai Indians' dreams of IPL 2026 playoff qualification are hanging by a thread following an emphatic eight-wicket defeat to the Chennai Super Kings. The alarming trend of middle-order collapses, most recently in the battle against CSK, has been the story of MI this IPL 2026, and that has now propelled the 5-time champions into a do-or-die situation.

title: 'If they misbehave…': Trump warns of fresh strikes, says he wants to 'eliminate' Iran's missile capability
content: President Trump has indicated fresh military strikes against Iran are possible if Tehran "misbehaves," even as he reviews a new 14-point peace proposal. The US leader expressed doubt about the plan's acceptability, stating Iran hasn't paid a sufficient price for its past actions. Iran's proposal seeks a regional conflict resolution within 30 days.

title: 11 dead in MP boat tragedy: Warnings ignored; cruise sailed in 50km/h winds, ended in sinking
content: The death toll in the Bargi dam cruise disaster has risen to 11 after two more children's bodies were recovered. The cruise reportedly launched despite wind warnings exceeding safety limits, contrasting with stricter practices elsewhere. Search operations continue for four missing passengers.

title: Pulled off train in January, Kuldeep Kumar breaks pole vault record five months later
content: Kuldeep Kumar shattered the national pole vault record, clearing 5.41m in Bhubaneswar, months after he and a teammate were controversially removed from a train for carrying their poles. The 21-year-old's impressive performance, a personal best and his first indoor competition, surpassed the previous mark set by Dev Kumar Meena. Both athletes are now focused on future major events.

title: ‘Not yet paid a big enough price’: Trump sceptical as Iran submits 14-point plan to end war
content: US President Trump is reviewing a new Iranian proposal for a comprehensive peace framework, but expressed significant doubt about its acceptance. He believes Iran hasn't sufficiently "paid a price" for past actions. The plan reportedly seeks security guarantees, withdrawal of US forces, and sanctions relief. Trump remains cautious, hinting at potential military action if necessary.

title: CSK youngster recreates Dhoni's 'gunshot' celebration vs Mumbai Indians
content: Young Kartik Sharma channelled MS Dhoni's iconic celebration after hitting the winning runs for Chennai Super Kings against Mumbai Indians.  Sharma's unbeaten 54 secured an eight-wicket victory for CSK. He revealed the planned gesture was for his first IPL fifty, a milestone he achieved with a composed knock. The win propelled CSK up the standings.

title: Sudesh Kumar, hero of ‘Saranga’ and producer of ‘Uljhan’, passes away
content: Veteran actor and producer Sudesh Kumar, known for his roles in 'Saranga' and 'Chhoti Bahen', passed away at 95 in Mumbai. Kumar, who also produced thrillers like 'Uljhan', began his career in theatre before making a mark in films. His wife confirmed his passing after a brief illness. He was born in Peshawar and later moved to Bombay.

title: Arvid Lindblad interview: Playing cricket at Mumbai maidans, Indian roots, F1 journey
content: Arvid Lindblad made an impressive Formula One debut with a points finish but also revealed a strong connection to India and cricket. During his Mumbai visit, he played street cricket at maidans linked to Sachin Tendulkar and said he might have pursued the sport if not racing, highlighting his Indian roots and sporting versatility beyond F1.

title: 30-year-old judge found dead at Delhi home
content: On Saturday, a somber shadow fell over South Delhi as 30-year-old Aman Sharma, a dedicated judge and secretary of the District Legal Services Authority, took his own life at his home. Family alarms raised, prompting police intervention; emergency teams had to force entry into his locked bathroom to determine his condition.

title: 'Pehle Rohit bhai, phir Dhoni bhai ... ': Surya reveals story behind iconic pic
content: Suryakumar Yadav shared an iconic photo with Rohit Sharma and MS Dhoni, sparking fan excitement and a unique "CUP-TAANS" suggestion from the BCCI. Yadav described the moment as a dream come true, reflecting on leading India to a T20 World Cup victory in 2026, a feat previously achieved by Dhoni and Sharma.

title: Vrabel marriage on brink as counselling fails amid Russini drama
content: Mike Vrabel and Jen Vrabel are reportedly undergoing marriage counselling as fallout from the Dianna Russini controversy strains their relationship. Sources say Vrabel is dealing with intense emotions and focusing on work while trying to repair the situation. Jen feels embarrassed but hopes to save the marriage. Despite efforts to reconcile, insiders suggest the situation may not be resolved quickly.

title: Maria Sharapova net worth 2026: Grand Slam icon’s empire grows
content: Maria Sharapova, a tennis icon, amassed an estimated $180 million net worth primarily through lucrative endorsement deals and her candy brand, Sugarpova. Her remarkable career included winning all four Grand Slams and reaching world No. 1. After retiring in 2020, she continues to thrive in business and investments, maintaining her global recognition.

title: Bagri dam tragedy: 3 of family return Delhi from Madhya Pradesh's Jabalpur in coffins
content: What began as a picturesque family getaway at Madhya Pradesh's Bargi dam quickly turned into a heartbreaking disaster. A tragic cruise boat capsized, pulling three innocent lives into its depths: Marina Massey, her beloved mother Madhur, and her young son Trishaan, just four years old.

title: 'Raja Shivaji' BO day 2 (Hindi): Riteish's film earns Rs 3.40 crore, total crosses Rs 21 crore
content: Riteish Deshmukh's labor of love, 'Raja Shivaji,' has struck a chord with film enthusiasts, portraying the iconic Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj. The film’s Marathi iteration has stormed the box office, while its Hindi counterpart is also seeing success. Despite competition from Akshay Kumar's 'Bhooth Bangla', and the Hollywood releases - 'Michael' and 'The Devil Wears Prada 2', the Riteish Deshmukh starrer is dominating the box office. Read on to know more.

title: 'Raja Shivaji' collection day 2: Riteish Deshmukh’s epic saga crosses Rs 15cr mark
content: Riteish Deshmukh's historical drama 'Raja Shivaji' has achieved a record-breaking opening in Marathi, earning over Rs 15 crore in its first two days. The Hindi version also performed decently, pushing the combined India net collection to Rs 21.90 crore. The film is on track to reach Rs 30 crore by the end of its opening weekend.

title: West Bengal polls: EC scraps Falta election, orders repolling on May 21
content: Bengal's electoral landscape has shifted as the Election Commission cancelled polling in Falta constituency due to widespread EVM tampering. A repoll is set for May 21, making it a unique post-result election. Allegations of threats against villagers and the arrest of a candidate's aides have heightened tensions, focusing attention on the remaining seats to decide the state's next government.

title: ‘You are just fighting among yourselves’: Babar Azam hits out at 90s Pakistan cricketers
content: Pakistan cricket faces internal strife post-T20 World Cup exit. Player Shadab Khan's spat with former stars Shahid Afridi and Mohammad Yousuf over criticism highlighted a growing divide. Captain Babar Azam has now called for mutual respect, acknowledging players' right to defend themselves against personal attacks while urging support for the national team.

title: West Bengal elections: Ahead of counting, TMC & BJP spar over EVM security
content: Ahead of Bengal's vote counting, Trinamool and BJP traded accusations over EVM strongroom security. TMC alleged CCTV blackouts in North 24 Parganas, while BJP pointed to alleged unauthorized access and attempted breaches in Salt Lake and Burdwan. Both parties raised concerns about the integrity of the storage facilities.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ mints over Rs 1785 cr as ‘Raja Shivaji’ takes lead on Friday
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its steady box office run, grossing over Rs 1785 crore globally after 44 days. Despite facing new competition, including Riteish Deshmukh's 'Raja Shivaji' which took the lead on Friday, 'Dhurandhar 2' added Rs 1.05 crore on its 7th Friday, bringing its India net collection to Rs 1,136.14 crore.

title: Phone sirens startle millions as government rolls out real-time warning system
content: A nationwide test of a new cell broadcast disaster warning system caused widespread confusion and alarm across India Saturday morning.  Phones blared "Extremely Severe Alert" messages, startling commuters, theatre-goers, and patients. While intended to test reach and speed, the drill highlighted the need for clearer communication about such vital emergency alerts.

title: Ruturaj Gaikwad masterclass sinks MI as CSK keep playoff hopes alive
content: Chennai Super Kings secured a vital eight-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians, bolstered by a disciplined bowling attack led by Noor Ahmad and Anshul Kamboj. Ruturaj Gaikwad's unbeaten 67 anchored the chase, with strong support from Kartik Sharma, ensuring CSK's playoff hopes remain alive while pushing MI further down the table.

title: 21-yr-old UP woman burnt alive by lover, his kin; dies at Delhi hospital with 90% burns
content: A 21-year-old woman, Kashish Meena, tragically died after battling severe burn injuries for a week. She was allegedly set ablaze by her lover and his family following a relationship dispute. Police have registered a murder case against the accused, including the lover who has been arrested. An investigation is underway, with authorities also probing potential negligence by local police.

title: Army starts process to procure modern bullet-proof troop carriers for Rashtriya Rifles
content: The Indian Army is set to acquire 159 bullet-proof troop carriers for its Rashtriya Rifles battalions operating in Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh. These vehicles will enhance protected mobility in challenging terrains, supporting counter-insurgency operations with improved troop transportation and protection. The procurement aligns with the 'Make in India' initiative, aiming for domestic production.

title: In Gujarat, bouncers hired to watch screen addicts
content: Desperate parents in Gujarat are hiring bouncers to manage teenagers battling severe phone, gaming, and drug addictions.  These security personnel, costing up to Rs 65,000 monthly, are deployed 24/7 to prevent violence, substance abuse, and risky behavior, highlighting a growing trend of extreme de-addiction measures.

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: 'Ten lifetimes won't be enough', Abhishek Banerjee challenges BJP to contest from Falta
content: Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee strongly responded to remarks by BJP leader Amit Malviya over ECI decision to order fresh polling in the Falta Assembly constituency, defending what he described as the "Diamond Harbour model crumbles"

title: Assam polls verdict on May 4; counting to decide BJP hat-trick or Congress comeback
content: EVMs, holding the electoral fates of 722 hopefuls from 126 Assembly constituencies of the State, will be opened at 40 counting centres across all 35 districts amid tight security arrangements

title: ‘Ten lifetimes won’t be enough’: TMC’s Abhishek Banerjee dares ‘bangla birodhi’ leaders to contest from Falta
content: Come to Falta. Send your strongest, send one of the godfathers from Delhi. If you have got the nerve, contest in Falta, says TMC general secretary Abhishek Banerjee

title: Opposing campaigns in Kerala map out the best- and worst-case scenarios for their respective alliances
content: Exit polls favouring UDF leave the Opposition feeling bullish. LDF feels voters will stay the course as in the past Assembly elections, preferring “stability over volatility”. The BJP-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is hoping to shrink the UDF and LDF’s margins by positioning itself as a Central government-backed development alternative to the traditional fronts in Kerala.

title: Watch: What happens on counting day? | Explained
content:
Voting is over. Across Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Puducherry, West Bengal and Assam, the big question now is: who wins? 
With counting of votes set to begin on May 4, here’s a closer look at how the process is done in India — step by step.

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Election is not a boxing contest, but a fight between ideologies: Riyas
content: Responding to UDF candidate P.V. Anvar’s dare to shave his head if defeated in the election, Minister says even UDF workers will not accept such challenges

title: Silence, the court is in session
content: The resignation of Allahabad High Court judge Yashwant Varma last month, amid parliamentary proceedings to remove him over allegations that burnt wads of unaccounted cash were found at his Delhi residence in 2025, has brought into focus how the judiciary holds itself to account. Aaratrika Bhaumik reports on the opacity surrounding complaints against judges, the routine rejection of information requests, and the shrinking space for criticism

title: 83.96% men, 86.2% women voted at polling stations in Assembly poll across Tamil Nadu: EC
content: A total of 4,87,98,833 voters out of 5,73,43,291 turned up to exercise their franchise on April 23 in T.N. Assembly polls; total votes polled may go up as the current figures do not include postal ballots or senior citizens who voted through Form 12D

title: Delhi records minimum temperature of 24.6°C, thunderstorms with rain likely
content: The IMD on Saturday (May 2, 2026) issued a 'yellow' alert for the national capital for the next two days, warning of rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds. The cloudy skies and intermittent rain are expected to persist till May 6

title: EC makes final preparations for counting of votes at 62 centres across Tamil Nadu
content: Chief Electoral Officer Archana Patnaik holds review meeting with with Election Commission officials ahead of counting day on May 4

title: MP boat tragedy: Death toll climbs to 13; body of last missing person recovered
content: The boat, around 20 years old and operated by the State tourism department, capsized during a storm on April 30, 2026

title: West Bengal election: Hundreds stage protest over ‘threats’ by TMC workers in Falta
content: The CRPF deputy commandant visited the area with a large force and assured the demonstrators of ensuring their safety.

title: High turnout for repoll in 15 West Bengal booths; peaceful amid tight security
content: Protests erupt in Falta over BJP’s allegations of malpractice and voter harassment, with a decision on repolls in the seat still pending

title: India’s first State-led Centre of Excellence for space tech launched in Bengaluru
content: The idea is to drive advanced research and innovation in space technologies, industry-aligned training and talent development, startup incubation and ecosystem building and cross-sector applications of

title: How to see election results of Assembly polls 2026? A step-by-step guide
content: Approximately 15.93 crore people have voted across the places going to polls, with West Bengal recording a voter turnout of 92.47%, its highest since India’s independence.

title: SCR seeks land at four locations in Telangana for mega coaching terminals
content: Key infrastructure bottlenecks identified at a meeting includes long-pending proposal to widen the vent on Tank Bund–Ranigunj road, construction of an additional ROB in Mallapur

title: Elephant parading rules violated rampantly, alleges animal task force
content: Activists blame systemic lapses for deaths in Irinjalakuda and Angamaly; demand strict enforcement of norms

title: Tribals, Scheduled Castes preserved India's identity and soul despite foreign invasions: Bhagwat
content: "This is the society's enduring ethos, which has survived for thousands of years. For various reasons, partly because of our indifference and partly because of foreign invasion, those who preserved this ethos paid a heavy price," says Mohan Bhagwat

title: News in Frames: Ban season, but the catch holds
content: Even as the annual fishing ban remains in force, some fishermen are sustaining livelihoods within permitted limits; using small fish as bait, they are landing high-value catch and earning during the ban

title: High speed mechanised boats proposed for Nellore fishermen
content: The absence of such larger boats along the State’s coastline is widely felt as the reason for poaching from the neighbouring States

title: Rahul Gandhi cites Gujarat BJP chief’s ‘indecent remarks’ against Geniben Thakor
content: Mr. Gandhi says the controversy exposes the BJP’s anti-women and Manuwadi mindset and questions the party’s commitment to empowering women

title: Access to generic Risdiplam sought under policy for rare diseases for people with Spinal Muscular Atrophy
content: The recent availability of India-manufactured generic Risdiplam offers a practical pathway, patients write in an appeal to the PM; access to treatment is time sensitive, with delays having the potential to cause irreversible deterioration in the life-limiting neuromuscular condition

title: Jabalpur cruise tragedy: Death toll rises to 11 as bodies of two kids found; search continues
content: Two more missing people from the capsized cruise boat are yet to be found; M.P. Tourism Secretary says a revised SOP will be prepared to prevent such incidents in future

title: Stuck for over an hour, no response from police, says woman who confronted Maharashtra Minister for traffic jam
content: Teena Choudhry, the woman who confronted Maharashtra Minister Girish Mahajan over a traffic jam, has released an account of the incident, saying said she was stuck near Mahindra Taaz for more than an hour and received no response from police officers

title: Social security measures for construction workers soon, says A.P. Minister
content: Proposals have been submitted for a 500-bed secondary hospital and a 150-bed superspecialty ESIC medical college in Amaravati, says Subhash

title: Raghav Chadha | The prodigy who walked away
content: The Rajya Sabha MP, who was one of the close confidants of Kejriwal, now says the ‘toxic work culture’ of AAP forced him to quit the party and join the BJP

title: Court denies bail to woman accused in TCS Nashik case
content: The accused is now likely to appeal against the decision in the Bombay High Court. Four others arrested in the case have been remanded to police custody till May 5

title: ECI announces repolling for Falta Assembly seat in West Bengal on May 21
content: In a notification, the ECI says multiple complaints of electoral malpractices were received on the day of polling in the constituency. The votes will be counted on May 24

title: 9-year-old mountaineer from Siddipet scales Australia’s highest peak
content: Vihaan undertook the expedition with a social message, carrying a banner urging youth to ‘Say No to Drugs’

title: Sringeri seat recount: Raje Gowda’s vote tally reduced by 255; BJP supporters celebrate
content: The Returning Officer has stated that he submitted the report about verification and recount to the Election Commission

title: Karnataka police told not to summon women at night; enforce KSHRC guidelines
content: In emergency situations where questioning is necessary at night, the presence of a woman police officer is mandatory

title: 12.38 lakh tonnes paddy purchased from 1.54 lakh ryots so far, ₹1,129 crore paid
content: According to Commissioner of Civil Supplies M. Stephen Ravindra, the department is operating at full capacity across the State to handle the inflow of paddy

title: Akhilesh Yadav takes jibe on Uttar Pradesh Deputy CMs, duo hit back
content: SP chief Akhilesh Yadav took a swipe at Uttar Pradesh Deputy CMs following their public appearance together

title: Panel visits Rajasthan town for probe into clash between BJP MLA, officials
content: Senior IAS officer Ravi Jain said that the committee would submit a detailed report to Chief Minister Bhajan Lal Sharma

title: Ernakulam sees heavy inflow of suspected synthetic drugs
content: The spike follows a crackdown triggered by a raid at a star hotel at Kadavanthra in March, where eight professionals were arrested with suspected narcotic substances

title: Assembly elections: ECI orders another round of repoll in all booths of Bengal's Falta seat on May 21 | Highlights
content: Demanding safety after counting of votes, hundreds of villagers staged a protest in Falta in West Bengal’s South 24 Parganas district, alleging they were receiving life threats from TMC workers in the area

title: India at Venice Biennale 2026 curtain raiser: Many voices, one resonance
content: From soil and thread to immersive myth and monumental sculpture, India’s Venice Biennale 2026 presence expands beyond the pavilion into a layered meditation on home and identity

title: Tele-MANAS helpline counselling 3,000 people per month in A.P., says Health Minister
content: Tele-MANAS centres in Vijayawada and Visakhapatnam, which were opened in 2022 and 2023 respectively, have received 67,573 calls so far

title: Aqua farmers in upbeat mood as India’s seafood exports cross ₹72,000-crore mark
content: Farmers request the Centre to have a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) with more countries to reduce the dependence on the U.S.A.

title: 19 years on, demand for direct train from Vizag to Bengaluru remains unfulfilled
content: With many IT giants investing in Visakhapatnam region and several big-ticket projects in pipeline, the necessity of a direct train connecting the two top IT destinations of India is being felt more than ever before

title: "Wife Was Shouting, He Was Crying": Family Of Delhi Judge Who Died By Suicide
content: The judicial officer's family has claimed he had been under distress and had complained of harassment in the days leading up to his death.

title: How Indian Start-up's Drishti Satellite Could Be New Headache For Pak, China
content: Suyash Singh, CEO and Founder of GalaxEye, called the mission a technological first and explained why India needed to think differently while designing satellites.

title: Indian Ship Carrying LPG Transits Hormuz In A Rare Crossing Amid US Blockade
content: The Marshall Islands-flagged Sarv Shakti - laden with around 45,000 tons of LPG, commonly used as cooking fuel - appeared to move into the Gulf of Oman after sailing past Iran's Larak and Qeshm islands.

title: 40,000 Attend Illegal Rave Party On French Military Site
content: The gathering comes as the French parliament is seeking to tighten legislation against unauthorised rave parties, introducing prison sentences for organisers and fines for attendees.

title: Did AC Explosion Lead To Delhi Building Fire? What Residents Said
content: More than a dozen people were rescued from the building, officials said. The injured people have been taken to the Guru Teg Bahadur Hospital for treatment.

title: 3 Years After Manipur Ethnic Violence, Families Of Missing People Still Await Them
content: Year 3 since Manipur ethnic violence began: Families have asked the state government to fulfil its constitutional responsibility and ensure the cases are not forgotten

title: Charred Bodies, Debris: Video Shows Grim Aftermath Of Delhi Building Fire
content: The fire broke out on the second floor of the building around 4 am today, following which as many as 14 fire engines were rushed to the spot to put out the fire and carry out rescue operations.

title: Gurugram Man Poisons Wife, 4 Children, Then Tries To Kill Self
content: Police identified the accused as Nazim, a native of Moradabad, who had been running a salon in Gurugram.

title: Flier 'Vomited Twice' Before Jumping Off Moving Plane At Chennai Airport
content: An official said the passenger on the Sharjah-Chennai Air Arabia flight complained of a vomiting sensation and is believed to have vomited twice on board.

title: Analysis: Tamil Nadu Heads For Dravidian Shakeout After Assembly Poll Verdict
content: The only word that might connect all these forces competing today but shifting loyalties tomorrow in an uneasy coalition is the adjective"Dravidian".

title: EVMs Taped To Block Votes, Scent Smeared To Find Voters: Bengal Repoll Report
content: The EC report said allegations of widespread booth-capturing came, with reports of forceful takeover of polling stations where genuine voters were prevented from voting or coerced through threats and intimidation tactics

title: China Invokes First-ever "Blocking Order" Against US Sanctions: Report
content: The move specifically protects five major Chinese petrochemical firms recently targeted by Washington for their alleged involvement in the Iranian oil trade.

title: Four Dead After Family Rescue Attempt Goes Wrong At Gujarat's Aji Dam
content: Rajkot, May 2 (IANS) Four members of a family drowned in Aji Dam in Rajkot on Saturday after a visit to a riverside temple ended in a fatal sequence of rescue attempts, police said.​

title: Pinarayi Vijayan: The Leader Who Remade Kerala's Left
content: After he became Kerala's 12th chief minister in May 2016, Pinarayi Vijayan faced some of the hardest challenges of his political journey.

title: Jabalpur Cruise Boat Tragedy: Two More Bodies Recovered, Death Count At 11
content: CM Mohan Yadav said that the rescue operations are still underway and assured strict punishment to those responsible for the tragedy.

title: Woman Charged For Car Crash That Killed Indian-origin Schoolgirl In London
content: Claire Freemantle was involved in a collision at the Study Prep School in Wimbledon that led to the death of Indian-origin schoolgirl Nuria and her schoolmate Serena Lau, both eight years old at the time, in July 2023.

title: Indian-origin Meenu Batra released from ICE custody after months; living in US for 35 years
content: Meenu Batra At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: Newly-wed couple found dead at Hyderabad home, suicide suspected
content: a newly-wed couple was found dead, allegedly by suicide, at their residence in Kukatpally on Saturday . the couple was identified as Yemjala Karthik, 28, who worked in the marketing cell of a private company, and his wife, Yerramalla Manjula, 27, a homemaker .
